title: Global Markets React as US and Iran Reach Framework Peace Deal
subtitle: While the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz brings hope for lower energy costs, experts warn that a return to normal will be slow.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have announced a framework agreement to end their recent conflict. This deal aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and gas shipments. While oil prices have dropped following the announcement, experts caution that a full recovery will take months. Shipping companies remain careful as they work to move vessels trapped in the region. The global economy continues to face challenges as countries work to refill their energy supplies.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗宣布達成一項框架協議，以結束近期的衝突。此協議旨在重啟荷莫茲海峽，這是一條對全球石油與天然氣運輸至關重要的航道。儘管協議宣布後油價下跌，但專家提醒全面復甦仍需數月時間。航運公司在設法移動受困於該區域的船隻時仍保持謹慎。隨著各國致力於回補能源儲備，全球經濟仍面臨挑戰。

paragraphs.en: The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to end their recent war, offering a sign of relief to global energy markets. The conflict, which lasted over three months, caused one of the largest disruptions to energy supplies in history. By closing the Strait of Hormuz—a narrow waterway that carries about one-fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas—the fighting forced global prices for fuel to climb sharply. Following the announcement of the framework deal, the price of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, fell to around $83 per barrel, down from a peak of $120 during the height of the conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 美國與伊朗已達成一項初步協議，旨在結束近期的戰爭，這為全球能源市場帶來了緩解的跡象。這場持續超過三個月的衝突，造成了歷史上最嚴重的能源供應中斷之一。由於荷莫茲海峽這條承載全球約五分之一石油與天然氣運輸的狹窄水道遭到封鎖，戰事導致全球燃料價格急劇攀升。在該框架協議宣布後，作為全球油價基準的布蘭特原油價格，已從衝突高峰期的每桶一百二十美元，跌至每桶約八十三美元。

paragraphs.en: President Donald Trump shared the news on social media, encouraging ships to begin moving again. However, the reality on the water is more complex. According to BBC Business, ship-tracking data shows that traffic levels in the Strait of Hormuz remain low. While the deal is a positive step, the waterway has been closed to most traffic since late February. Hundreds of ships have been stuck in the Gulf, facing risks from sea mines and drone strikes. Shipping giants like Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd have reported that their vessels remain in the region, and they are waiting for safety assurances before moving forward.
paragraphs.zh: 川普 (Donald Trump) 總統在社群媒體上分享了這項消息，並鼓勵船隻恢復航行。然而，海上的實際情況更為複雜。根據英國廣播公司商業新聞 (BBC Business) 報導，船舶追蹤數據顯示，荷莫茲海峽的交通量依然偏低。儘管該協議是正面的一步，但這條水道自二月底以來便對多數船隻關閉。數百艘船隻受困於波斯灣，面臨水雷與無人機攻擊的風險。馬士基 (Maersk) 與赫伯羅特 (Hapag-Lloyd) 等航運巨頭表示，其船隻仍停留在該區域，正等待安全保證後才敢繼續前進。

paragraphs.en: Experts warn that even with a signed agreement, the process of returning to normal will be slow. Neil Shearing, the chief economist at Capital Economics, noted that it remains to be seen whether this deal is a temporary truce or a long-term solution. He explained that even if the route becomes safe, tankers are currently in the wrong places, and oil facilities need time to return to full production. Furthermore, the cost and availability of insurance for ships traveling through the area remain major concerns for shipping companies.
paragraphs.zh: 專家警告，即便簽署了協議，恢復正常的過程仍將緩慢。凱投宏觀 (Capital Economics) 的首席經濟學家尼爾·希林 (Neil Shearing) 指出，這項協議究竟是暫時的停火還是長期的解決方案，仍有待觀察。他解釋說，即便航道變得安全，油輪目前的位置也不正確，且石油設施需要時間才能恢復全面生產。此外，航經該區域船隻的保險成本與取得難度，仍是航運公司面臨的主要隱憂。

paragraphs.en: For the global economy, the impact of the war has been significant. During the conflict, countries around the world had to use their emergency oil stockpiles to make up for the lack of supply. According to the US Energy Information Administration, oil stocks fell at a rapid rate between March and May. While some countries, such as China, reduced their imports and used domestic supplies to keep prices in check, industry experts warn that these buffers are now reaching critical levels. Jorge Leon, an analyst at Rystad Energy, noted that while inventory releases provided short-term relief, they could not fully replace the lost flow from the Strait of Hormuz.
paragraphs.zh: 對全球經濟而言，這場戰爭的影響相當深遠。衝突期間，世界各國不得不動用緊急石油儲備來彌補供應短缺。根據美國能源資訊管理局 (EIA) 的數據，石油庫存量在三月至五月間迅速下降。雖然中國等部分國家減少了進口並使用國內儲備來抑制價格，但產業專家警告，這些緩衝存量已達臨界點。能源諮詢公司 Rystad Energy 的分析師豪爾赫·里昂 (Jorge Leon) 指出，雖然釋放庫存提供了短期緩解，但無法完全取代荷莫茲海峽中斷的供應量。

paragraphs.en: There are also political motivations behind the timing of this deal. In the United States, rising fuel prices have created a difficult situation for the Trump administration ahead of midterm elections. The Guardian reports that the deal is being presented as a victory to help lower gasoline prices for American consumers. For Iran, a gradual reopening is also seen as a tactical choice, allowing the government to maintain some influence during the upcoming 60-day negotiation period regarding its nuclear program.
paragraphs.zh: 這項協議的時機背後也有政治動機。在美國，燃料價格上漲已對川普政府在期中選舉前造成了艱困的局面。《衛報》(The Guardian) 報導稱，這項協議被塑造成一項政績，旨在協助降低美國消費者的汽油價格。對伊朗而言，逐步重啟航道也被視為一種戰術選擇，讓政府能在接下來關於其核子計畫的六十天談判期間，維持一定的影響力。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the path to stability is filled with challenges. The US and Iran are expected to sign the formal agreement this Friday. Following the signing, the process of clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz will begin, which could take up to seven weeks. Even if this process goes smoothly, market observers believe that prices may stay between $80 and $90 per barrel for the rest of the year as buyers rush to refill empty emergency reserves. Additionally, while oil flows may recover by the end of the third quarter, gas exports could take longer to stabilize due to damage caused to processing facilities during the conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，邁向穩定的道路充滿挑戰。美國與伊朗預計將於本週五簽署正式協議。簽署後，清除荷莫茲海峽水雷的作業將隨之展開，這可能需要長達七週的時間。即便過程順利，市場觀察家認為，由於買家急於回補空缺的緊急儲備，油價在今年剩餘時間內可能維持在每桶八十至九十美元之間。此外，雖然石油運輸可能在第三季末恢復，但由於加工設施在衝突中受損，天然氣出口可能需要更長時間才能穩定。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, while the announcement of a peace deal is a welcome development for the global economy, it is not an instant fix. The world will be watching closely to see how quickly the Strait of Hormuz can truly return to its role as a secure trade route. Until then, the global energy market remains in a state of careful recovery, with businesses and governments alike waiting for the first signs of a return to pre-war normality.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，雖然和平協議的宣布對全球經濟而言是受歡迎的進展，但這並非立即見效的解方。全球將密切關注荷莫茲海峽能以多快的速度真正恢復其安全貿易航道的角色。在此之前，全球能源市場仍處於謹慎復甦的狀態，企業與政府都在等待戰前常態回歸的第一個跡象。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to move or get away from a place.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常與動詞 stick 連用，這裡作為形容詞描述船隻無法移動的狀態。文中：Hundreds of ships have been stuck in the Gulf, facing risks from sea mines and drone strikes.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A state of control or restraint.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（檢查），這裡作名詞，指控制價格的手段。文中：While some countries, such as China, reduced their imports and used domestic supplies to keep prices in check, industry experts warn that these buffers are now reaching critical levels.
常見作動詞（修理），這裡作名詞，指問題的解決之道。文中：Ultimately, while the announcement of a peace deal is a welcome development for the global economy, it is not an instant fix.

previousArticles.title: Global Markets React as US and Iran Reach Framework Peace Deal
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United States and Iran have reached a framework agreement to end their recent conflict. This deal aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and gas shipments. While oil prices have dropped following the announcement, experts caution that shipping will not return to normal immediately. Many vessels remain stuck in the region, and clearing the waterway of mines is a complex task. Global markets are watching closely to see if this agreement leads to a lasting peace.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason why shipping companies are not immediately resuming travel through the Strait of Hormuz despite the peace deal?

options.A: They are waiting for the official signing ceremony on Friday.
options.B: They require safety assurances due to the presence of mines and drones.
options.C: They are currently under government orders to keep their vessels in the Gulf.
options.D: They are waiting for global oil prices to stabilize below $80 per barrel.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確指出，儘管有和平協議，但貨輪仍留在該地區，是因為船運巨頭（如 Maersk 和 Hapag-Lloyd）正在等待安全保證（safety assurances），以應對水雷和無人機攻擊的風險。選項 A 錯誤，因為簽署儀式是後續步驟，並非船隻不敢移動的主因。選項 C 錯誤，文章未提及政府強迫船隻滯留。選項 D 錯誤，油價並非船運公司決策的主要考量因素。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the global oil supply situation following the end of the conflict?

options.A: The global market will likely experience an immediate surplus of oil.
options.B: Countries will no longer need to rely on their emergency oil stockpiles.
options.C: The recovery of the oil supply will be hindered by depleted national reserves and logistical issues.
options.D: Oil production will return to pre-war levels as soon as the Strait of Hormuz is cleared of mines.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段與第四段的敘述，即便航道重啟，油輪位置錯誤、設施需要維修，且各國的緊急儲備已降至臨界點（critical levels），這意味著供應恢復將面臨多重阻礙。選項 A 錯誤，市場短期內仍處於恢復期，不會立即過剩。選項 B 錯誤，文章提到各國仍需回填儲備，因此仍依賴儲備管理。選項 D 錯誤，文章提到油氣設施受損，恢復生產需要時間，並非清雷後就能立即恢復。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the word 'buffers' refer to in the context of the global oil market?

options.A: Emergency oil stockpiles used to mitigate supply shortages.
options.B: Protective barriers installed around oil processing facilities.
options.C: Negotiation periods between opposing political factions.
options.D: Financial insurance policies for shipping companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第四段中，作者提到各國在衝突期間使用了 emergency oil stockpiles（緊急石油儲備）來彌補供應不足，隨後提到這些「buffers」已達到臨界點。這裡的 buffers 指的就是這些作為緩衝的儲備資源。選項 B 錯誤，文中未提及物理防護設施。選項 C 錯誤，這是指政治談判。選項 D 錯誤，保險問題在第三段討論，與此處的儲備存量無關。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the recent US-Iran peace deal?

options.A: The deal ensures an immediate return to global economic prosperity.
options.B: The agreement is primarily a political strategy to influence upcoming elections.
options.C: While the deal is a positive development, significant practical and logistical challenges remain before normalcy is achieved.
options.D: The conflict has permanently altered the role of the Strait of Hormuz in the global energy market.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章整體論述了和平協議雖然是個好消息，但作者透過多個段落強調了實際面臨的挑戰，如清雷過程、設施損壞、儲備不足以及船運公司的安全疑慮，顯示恢復過程將會很緩慢。選項 A 過於樂觀，與文章強調的「not an instant fix」矛盾。選項 B 僅提及部分政治背景，無法涵蓋全文關於能源市場恢復的討論。選項 D 錯誤，文中並未斷言航道的角色已被永久改變。
